title: Establishing qPCR Reference Standards for Halal Food Testing
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Halal   authentication     is     a     growing     priority     for   global   food     industries  ,   requiring     robust     and     reliable   testing   methods  .   Quantitative   PCR (  qPCR  )   offers     a     rapid     and     highly     specific     tool     for     detecting   non-halal   contaminants     such   as   porcine   DNA in   meat  , gelatin,   and     processed     foods  .   However  ,   the     absence     of     internationally     harmonized     reference     standards     can     lead     to     inconsistent     results     across     laboratories  ,   undermining   halal   certification     credibility  .   Establishing     validated     qPCR     reference     standards     will     provide     a   uniform   benchmark     for     sensitivity  ,   specificity  ,   and     reproducibility   in halal testing.   This     policy     brief     calls     for     coordinated     efforts     between     regulatory     bodies  , halal   certification     agencies  ,   and     laboratories     to     develop     and     implement     standardized     reference     materials  ,   ensuring     transparency  ,   comparability  ,   and     consumer     trust   in halal   food     assurance     programs     worldwide  .
